prompt: Camera orbit left. An alpine cirque lake sits beneath steep granite walls streaked with old snow and dark mineral seams. Near the shore, pale stones are visible through clear green water, then the bottom drops sharply to a cobalt center. Small wind ripples disturb a near-perfect reflection of the peaks, while a feeder waterfall enters from a cleft in the rock, breaking into mist and white spray before spreading into overlapping rings. Fallen trunks, wet boulders, and a narrow crescent of gravel define the shoreline, and thin cloud shadow passes across the basin, changing the water from glassy mirror to textured surface.

Camera orbit right. A broad tundra valley in late autumn stretches toward distant mountains, its braided river already edged with thin new ice. Frost clings to ochre grasses and low shrubs, while darker patches of unfrozen ground absorb the last warmth of the day. Overhead, a layered sky carries a snow squall on one side and a clear amber break on the other, creating a precise transition from cold blue shade to low golden light across the terrain. Gusts lift loose crystals along the riverbanks, clouds thicken around the peaks, and the first sparse flakes begin to cross the open air as the storm line advances.

Camera pan left. A desert badlands escarpment reveals stacked sedimentary history in crisp horizontal bands of rust, tan, ash gray, and chalk white. The foreground shelf is fractured into polygonal plates and narrow gullies, leading to tall hoodoos, tilted strata, and a natural stone arch eroded from a more resistant caprock layer. Farther back, a dry wash snakes between buttes toward a distant mesa softened by heat haze. Every surface shows plausible weathering: talus cones gathered beneath cliffs, undercut alcoves where softer layers retreated, and sharp joint lines catching low sun that sculpts the relief with long, readable shadows.

Camera pan right. A cold coastal inlet at dawn curves between dark basalt headlands, with a band of black sand and rounded tide pools exposed by the receding sea. In the shallows, translucent water slides over rippled sand bars and submerged kelp, revealing green-brown fronds swaying beneath the surface before the next set arrives. Each wave folds into white lace against the rocks, sending brief fans of spray into the air and leaving reflective sheets that mirror the brightening sky. Drift logs, barnacled stones, and thin foam trails mark the shoreline, while offshore stacks emerge from sea mist and their reflections break apart in the restless water.
